name: Model Context Protocol
allowed_use_boundary: Use as protocol and tool-contract crosswalk evidence.
Do not treat capability discovery or invocation preparation as execution authority.
claims: MCP defines client-server contracts for context, resources, prompts, tools, capability negotiation, and authorization guidance.
non_claims: MCP does not prove transition admissibility.
MCP capability advertisement does not establish current delegation.
MCP tool invocation preparation does not grant consequence-binding authority.

transition_table_mapping.claims: context, resource, prompt, tool, capability-negotiation, and authorization-guidance protocol
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no standing, delegation, admissibility, or execution authority
transition_table_mapping.input_artifact_type: MCP capability, resource, prompt, or tool contract
transition_table_mapping.output_artifact_type: protocol exchange or proposed invocation evidence
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: client-server identities and advertised capabilities; authority not inherited
transition_table_mapping.evidence_model: official specification plus local bounded crosswalk
transition_table_mapping.delegation_model: must be reconstructed independently for the transition
transition_table_mapping.receipt_or_trace_model: protocol messages and local compatibility record
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: source, contract, and exchange evidence can reconstruct protocol context
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: tool and resource evidence may enter standing review
transition_table_mapping.StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap: preparation and interoperability path before commitment
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: missing source version, undefined capability mapping, missing delegation, or authority overclaim

fail_closed_conditions: source reference or version is missing
delegation or authority is inferred from capability advertisement
comparative behavior is claimed without executable evidence
